truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 9 vulnerabilities. It fully matched 6 of the 20 true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001, SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-006, SC-V3-009, SC-V3-010) with exact code anchors. The remaining 14 true vulnerabilities were missed. All 9 findings in Run 2 were supported by the source code, yielding no false positives. Severity ratings for three findings were underestimated compared to the ground truth.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string injection in the logging function using the exact anchor printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input).
Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation loop (result *= i).
Run 2 did not identify the use-after-free risk in stage_deferred_history_flush and memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Command Injection in Config Validator", "evidence": "int result = system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified command injection via unsanitized config validation passing directly to system().

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Off-by-One Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression", "evidence": "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow/strcpy vulnerability in input processing matching the anchor.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter", "evidence": "long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the race condition in the thread-safe counter where the read occurs outside the lock.
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb bypass in authentication using EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in configuration loading via config_dir_ + filename.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser", "evidence": "if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap overflow in the expression parser copying unbounded input into a fixed 256-byte buffer.
Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded administrator credentials matching the exact anchor.
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG usage for session tokens.
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging.
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling in the audit logging function.
Run 2 did not identify the persistent authentication state issue.
Run 2 did not identify unhandled exceptions during variable parsing via std::stod.
Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in the memory-pool block split logic.
Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in the parser.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in the result cache.
Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU vulnerability in configuration loading.
Run 2 did not identify the integer division by zero in command routing.

Items.Key: format string vulnerability in format_string|format string|cwe-134|high|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|221|221|string_utils::format_string|snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...);|if fmt is derived from untrusted input, attackers can cause information disclosure or application crashes via format specifiers.|untrusted format string passed to format_string|ensure fmt is a compile-time constant or strictly validated. use std::format or ensure user input is never used as the format string.->format string vulnerability in format_string|format string|cwe-134|high|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|221|221|string_utils::format_string|snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...);|if fmt is derived from untrusted input, attackers can cause information disclosure or application crashes via format specifiers.|untrusted format string passed to format_string|ensure fmt is a compile-time constant or strictly validated. use std::format or ensure user input is never used as the format string.

command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to complete host compromise.|executing an admin command prefixed with 'exec'|implement a strict command parser with a whitelist of allowed operations. replace system() with safe execution apis.->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to complete host compromise.|executing an admin command prefixed with 'exec'|implement a strict command parser with a whitelist of allowed operations. replace system() with safe execution apis.

off-by-one buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|832|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|when expression length exactly matches buffer size, strcpy writes the null terminator out-of-bounds, corrupting adjacent memory.|input expression length exactly equal to 1024 bytes|change condition to `<` or use strncpy/strlcpy, ensuring space for the null terminator.->off-by-one buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|when expression length exactly matches buffer size, strcpy writes the null terminator out-of-bounds, corrupting adjacent memory.|input expression length exactly equal to 1024 bytes|change condition to `<` or use strncpy/strlcpy, ensuring space for the null terminator.
